author: govinda dāsa
verses: tuhū se rahili madhupura vrajakula ākula, dukūla kalarava, kānu kānu kori jhura jaśomatī⋅nanda, andha sama baiṭhai, sāhase uṭhai nā pāra sakhā⋅gaṇa dhenu, veṇurava nā śuniye, vichurala nagara bāyāra kusuma tyajiyā ali, kṣititale luṭata, taru⋅gaṇa malina samāna mayūrī nā nācata, kapotī nā bolata, kokilā nā karatahi gāṇa virahiṇī rāī, virahajvare jara jara, caudike viraha hutāśa sahaje jamunā jala, āgi samāna bhela, kahatahi govinda dāsa
en_translation:
You have remained in Mathurā. The residents of Vraja are distressed, crying out and lamenting, "Kānu! Kānu!"

Yaśodā and Nanda sit as if blind, unable to find the courage to rise. The cowherd friends and the cows are desolate; the sound of the flute is not heard, and the city marketplace is empty.

The bees, abandoning the flowers, roll on the ground, and the trees look sad. The peahens do not dance, the doves do not coo, and the cuckoos do not sing.

Rādhā is wasting away with the fever of separation; the fire of separation is everywhere. Even the waters of the Yamunā have become like fire—so says Govinda dāsa.
